briefdescription.para: DMA0 type.
NIST start padding mask.
NIST start padding mask.
NIST start padding mask.
NIST end padding mask.
Maximum padding length for SHA.
SHA2-256 length field size.
SHA2 384 and 512 length field size.
Padding byte for SHA.
Bytes to bits conversion value.
SHA2 384 and 512 block length.
SHA2 256 block length.
SHA3 384 block length.
SHAKE SHA3 256 block length.
SHA3 512 block length.
One byte shift value for an integer.
Mask to extract least significant byte from integer values.
Start index for big-endian byte processing.
SHA platform configuration.
This function check whether hash calculation is completed or not.

listitem.para: Check for SHA operation is completed with in Timeout(10sec) or not

briefdescription.para: This function performs NIST padding for different SHA algorithms according to their respective specifications and transfers the combined partial data + padding to the SHA engine via DMA.

para.verbatim: Any data buffered in PartialData from XSecure_ShaDmaXfer is kept in place and the NIST padding is appended directly after it. The combined buffer is then DMA'd as a single transfer.

listitem.para: XSECURE_SHA_INVALID_PARAM - Upon invalid SHA algorithm parameter.

simplesect.para: This function applies algorithm-specific NIST padding: SHA2 - 0x80 start byte + zero padding + message length in bits (big-endian) SHA3 - 0x06 start byte + zero padding + 0x80 end byte SHAKE - 0x1F start byte + zero padding + 0x80 end byte

listitem.para: Determine LengthFieldSize based on hash algorithm
Zeroize the padding area in PartialData after the buffered data
Reset PartialLen before sending, since the combined data is already assembled in PartialData and XSecure_ShaDmaXfer manages PartialLen internally on platforms that require staging.
Send combined partial data + NIST padding to SHA engine

memberdef.argsstring: (XSecure_Sha *const InstancePtr, u64 DataAddr, u32 Len, u32 IsLastUpdate)
memberdef.name: XSecure_ShaDmaTransfer

briefdescription.para: This function transfers data to the SHA engine using DMA and manages the complete transfer process including SSS configuration.

parameterdescription.para: - Address of the data to be transferred to SHA engine.
- Length of the data to be transferred in bytes.
- Flag indicating if this is the last data transfer.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - Upon successful data transfer completion.
XST_FAILURE - Upon any failure during the transfer process.
XSECURE_SHA_DMA_TRANSFER_ERROR - Upon any failure during the transfer process.
Configure the SSS for SHA2/3 hashing.

briefdescription.para: This function returns a reference to an XSecure_ShaConfig structure based on the unique device id.

parameterdescription.para: Unique device ID of the device for the lookup operation.

listitem.para: It returns CfgPtr which is a reference to a config record in the configuration table
It returns NULL if no match is found.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes SHA instance so that it is ready to be used.

listitem.para: Initializes the secure stream switch instance

briefdescription.para: This function validates the SHA Mode (refer XSecure_ShaMode enum) and configures SSS and start the SHA engine.

parameterdescription.para: - Indicates SHA3/SHA2 shall be operated in which sha mode that is SHA-384/256/512

listitem.para: XSECURE_SHA_INVALID_PARAM - Invalid parameter (InstancePtr is NULL or invalid SHA mode)
XSECURE_SHA_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - SHA engine is not in initialized state
Validate SHA state is initialized or not
Validate the SHA mode and initialize SHA instance based on SHA mode.
Select SHA Mode based on SHA type.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the block length for the given SHA algorithm.

simplesect.para: Block length in bytes, or 0 for invalid algorithm.

memberdef.argsstring: (XSecure_Sha *const InstancePtr, u64 DataAddr, const u32 DataSize)
memberdef.name: XSecure_ShaUpdate

briefdescription.para: This function updates input data to SHA Engine to calculate Hash.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the input data for hashing.
Size of the input data in bytes.

listitem.para: Validate SHA state is started or not.
If the input data is of 0 length, do not initiate DMA transfer
Transfer input data to SHA engine using DMA
Set UPDATE_DONE only when IsLastUpdate is TRUE and all data has been DMA'd (PartialLen == 0). If partial data remains, SW NIST padding in ShaFinish will handle the final transfer.
Set SHA2/3 under reset on failure condition

memberdef.argsstring: (XSecure_Sha *const InstancePtr, u64 HashAddr, u32 HashBufSize, u32 ReqHashSize)
memberdef.name: XSecure_ExtendedShaFinish

briefdescription.para: This function calculates and reads the final hash of input data with support for variable-length hash output.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the buffer where the final hash will be stored.
Size allocated for the hash buffer in bytes. Must be at least as large as ReqHashSize to avoid buffer overflow.
Requested hash size in bytes to be read from the SHA digest. Must not exceed XSECURE_MAX_HASH_SIZE_IN_BYTES.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - Upon successful hash calculation and retrieval.
XST_FAILURE - Upon failure during hash completion or digest read.
XSECURE_SHA_INVALID_PARAM - When InstancePtr is NULL or HashBufSize is less than ReqHashSize.
XSECURE_SHA_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - When SHA engine is in initialized state without any update.
XSECURE_SHA_NIST_PADDING_ERROR - When NIST padding operation fails.
Validate Hash buffer size to avoid buffer overflow.

inbodydescription.para:
Switch padding to SW based padding to address the following scenarios.
SHA zero-data length use case.
SHA Final call without preceding SHA update call with IsLast set to TRUE use case.
Perform NIST padding and send to SHA engine
Check the SHA2/3 DONE bit.
Read the Hash (word-aligned) and store in Hash Buffer.
Handle last partial word if present
Set SHA2/3 under reset.

briefdescription.para: This function calculates and reads the final hash of input data.

listitem.para: Validate Hash buffer size to avoid buffer overflow.

inbodydescription.para:
Switch padding to SW based padding to address the following scenarios.
SHA zero-data length use case.
SHA Final call without preceding SHA update call with IsLast set to TRUE use case.
Perform NIST padding and send to SHA engine
Check the SHA2/3 DONE bit.
Read out the Hash and store in Hash Buffer.
Set SHA2/3 under reset.

memberdef.argsstring: (XSecure_Sha *const InstancePtr, XSecure_ShaMode ShaMode, u64 DataAddr, u32 DataSize, u64 HashAddr, u32 HashBufSize)
memberdef.name: XSecure_ShaDigest

briefdescription.para: This function calculates the SHA2/3 digest on the given input data.

parameterdescription.para: Indicates SHA3/SHA2 shall be operated in which sha mode that is SHA-384/256/512
Pointer to the input data for hashing.
Size of the input data in bytes.

listitem.para: Validate the SHA mode and initialize SHA instance based on SHA mode.
Configure SSS and start the SHA engine.
Update input data to SHA Engine to calculate Hash.
Calculate and read the final hash of input data.

briefdescription.para: This function notifies the SHA driver at the end of the SHA data update.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - If last update can be accepted
XSECURE_SHA_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - If State mismatch is occurred

briefdescription.para: This file contains the implementation of the interface functions for SHA2/3 driver.

detaileddescription.para: Refer to the header file xsecure_sha.h for more detailed information.

para.preformatted: Ver Who Date Changes ----- ---- -------- ------------------------------------------------------- 5.4 kal 07/24/24 Initial release sk 08/29/24 Added support for SDT flow tri 03/01/25 Added SSS configuration for SHA3 hashing 5.6 aa 07/21/25 Typecast to essential datatype to avoid implicit conversions fix dereference before null check tus 08/06/25 Add support for zero data length SHA use case vss 09/02/25 Fixed GCC warnings 5.6 rpu 08/11/25 Added crypto check in XSecure_ShaStart. 5.7 tvp 02/23/26 Use XSecure_ShaPlatConfig, platform specific SHA configurations tvp 02/23/26 Add SHAKE256 SLH-DSA Chaining algorithm support tvp 02/23/26 Add XSecure_ExtendedShaFinish to support variable-length hash output tvp 04/15/26 Add SHA mode validation in XSecure_ExtendedShaFinish
